\begin{figure}[tbp]
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=0.92\linewidth]{figures/strands.png}
  \caption{The prismatic head's three arms, rendered exactly as the dashboard's bias/variance strand card. Each hair is one feature, morphing from a ring (its static-field phase) out to its (bias, variance) position with a helical twist; color runs blue (pure bias) to red (pure variance). Head A (left) is the bias arm: it reads a static field, so every hair stays blue - a constant structural vote. Head B (center) is the variance arm, whose input-conditional envelope lets hairs swing toward red - the data-dependent vote. Head C (right) is the pure-variance arm: it carries no static spectrum at all, so its hairs grow pure red from nothing - variance arriving from the opposite end of the decomposition. Each panel's subtitle lists that arm's head stack. Together they are the orthogonal bias/variance decomposition made architectural - convex and radial from the center, chaotic in the arms.}
  \label{fig:strands}
